title: Unify Team admission projection and scale-down decisions
acceptance: Team opens only when mutually exclusive file groups are at least two and no shared bottleneck file blocks the critical path.
Admission first collapses unnecessary roles, then selects the cheapest capability-qualified provider/model under data-policy and risk constraints.
Production/default requires projected fully-loaded cost ratio `<= 0.80`, time ratio `<= 0.75`, quality parity and no worse repair/residue; unknown workload classes are bounded experiments only.
Missing provider usage, stale pricing catalog or incomplete subscription allocation makes the projection promotion-ineligible.

Admission outputs bounded fan-out before execution: max worker count, provider quota/rate-limit probe result, per-worker and total spending ceilings, estimated queue wait, stop-loss threshold and downgrade route.
Admission rejects or downgrades Team when the fan-out cap, quota probe, pricing catalog or TeamRosterFingerprint cannot be produced deterministically.
Raw token ratio remains a diagnostic signal and cannot reject a cheaper qualified model solely because its tokens cost less but are more numerous.
Team automatically shrinks, changes model mix, or returns to single Agent when projected or actual cost/time can no longer meet the applicable threshold.
Every scale-down records optimization reason and next experiment target.
outOfScope: Unrelated refactors outside the declared scope.
rollbackNotes: Disable the new lane and keep the previous canonical behavior.
